title: Personal & Corporate Banking | MSME & Agri banking - IDBI Bank
content:
**Interest rates on the range of NRI products are listed below:**
Senior Citizens are not given the facility of additional interest rate on investment made in any NRI Fixed Deposit (NRE / NRO / RFC / FCNR).
# Non Resident Ordinary - NRO/ Non Resident (External) - NRE Term Deposit rates (Effective From: February 23, 2026)  
| >1 Year to 2 Years (Except 555 Days and 700 Days)  |  
| --- |  
  * Staff & Senior Citizen Rates are not applicable to NRO & NRE term Deposits.
  * All other features of term deposit as well as terms and conditions shall remain unchanged and are applicable for the above scheme also.
  * No interest will be paid on NRE deposits closed pre-maturely in less than 1 year.
  * Effective from June 10, 2024, the maximum Retail Term Deposit (RTD) threshold has been enhanced to < Rs 3 Crores, from previously < Rs 2 Crores, as per RBI directives. 


# Discontinuation of Special Non-Callable Option FD under RTD segment for all Tenures effective from June 18, 2025.   
 |  
|  |  
 |  
 |  
 |  
 |  
(The interest rate on SB accounts for balances up to Rs.500 Cr shall be effective from Aug 18, 2025.)
  * For more details on the MIBOR Linked Floating Interest rate, please contact your Relationship Manager or visit your nearest IDBI Bank Branch.


# Resident Foreign Currency – RFC Deposit rates (% p.a.) (Effective From: July 01, 2026 )  
| 6 Months 1 day < 1 Year  |  
| --- |  
**Interest rates on RFC deposits are subject to change on the 1st of every month and as per the guidelines issued by the Reserve Bank of India from time to time.**  
| IDBI FCNR (B) Special Opportunity Deposit w.e.f. June 20, 2026  |  
| --- |  
| Deposit amount more than 1 million and upto 5 million  |  
**IDBI FCNR (B) Special Opportunity Deposit available till September 30,2026**
i.e., no premature withdrawal is permitted for a period of 1 year from the date of opening of FCNR (B) Deposits.
# Foreign Currency Non Resident (Bank) - FCNR (B) deposit rates ( % p.a.) (Effective From: July 01, 2026 )  
| 1 yr to less than 2 yrs  |  
| --- |  
| 3 yrs to less than 4 yrs  |  
| 4 yrs to less than 5 yrs  |  
| For Deposit amount more than USD 1 million and upto USD 5 million  | For Deposit amount more than USD 5 million  |  
| --- | --- |  
| 1 yr to less than 2 yrs  |  
| 3 yrs to less than 4 yrs  |  
| 4 yrs to less than 5 yrs  |  
  * Interest rates on RFC and FCNR(B) deposits are subject to change on the 1st of every month and as per the guidelines issued by the Reserve Bank of India from time to time. 
  * No interest will be paid on FCNR deposits closed pre-maturely in less than 1 year.
  * FCNR(B) deposit above USD 1 million or equivalent should be placed with prior consultation of Branch or NRI Cell at 
A. Penalty shall be levied on the premature withdrawal of FCNR(B) deposits when the depositors return to India for permanent settlement. B. Penalty shall also be levied on premature withdrawal for conversion of FCNR(B) deposits into NRE deposits or vice versa. A penalty will be charged by the Bank, on the premature withdrawal of FCNR(B) deposits (currently at 1%) on the applicable Rate of Interest (ROI) or the Contracted Deposit Interest Rate for the respective currency, whichever is lower shall be applicable in the above two scenarios( A & B). 

# Systematic Savings Plan- (Rebranding of Recurring Deposit) (Effective From: February 23, 2026 )  
Interest payable on prematurely withdrawn deposits will be the rate applicable for the amount and the period for which the deposit remained with the Bank (rate applicable for that tenure on the original date of the deposit). To illustrate as an example, if a deposit is placed for 5 years and after 1 year the depositor wants to close the deposit prematurely, then the interest rate applied will be the rate which was applicable for one year on the original date of deposit.
With the revision in the premature closure policy, Bank will now levy a penalty of 1%, on the applicable rate for deposits in RTD & RD closed prematurely. Such closures include the withdrawals through sweep-ins and partial withdrawals as well.
Disclaimer: Interest rates on FCNR and RFC deposits are subject to change on the 1st of every month and as per the guidelines issued by the Reserve Bank of India from time to time.
